meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest). Use `runtime.GOOS == "windows"` and `runtime.GOOS != "windows"` skips where needed. Never accept failing or flaky tests.

TDD ENFORCEMENT: The implementing agent MUST author comprehensive FAILING tests FIRST for each defect, THEN implement the fix. Tests must prove the defect exists (red) before the fix makes them pass (green). No implementing without tests first.

GNU Make is the build system. Use `make test`, `make lint`, `make build`. On macOS use `gmake` or confirm `make` is GNU Make. Single-package tests: `go test ./internal/scripting/... -run TestName -v`.
Tests must not mutate host system state. Use t.TempDir(), t.Setenv(), and mock external services. Never assume a specific home directory.

CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL FAILURE: jsModel.View() returns tea.NewView(viewStr) with zero-valued mode fields. cursedRenderer reads View.AltScreen, View.MouseMode, View.ReportFocus and sends escape sequences ONLY when non-zero. ALL tea.run({altScreen:true, mouse:true}) calls are SILENTLY IGNORED. See autopsy at ./scratch/autopsy-bubbletea-v2-20260407/02_mouse_mode_broken.md.

statusSection.currentState: Bubble Tea v2 migration — IN PROGRESS. Component level (textarea/viewport/key parsing/mouse message conversion/paste routing) is 100% complete. CRITICAL remaining work: wire tea.View mode fields in jsModel.View() — mouse, altScreen, and reportFocus are completely broken because jsModel.View() always returns tea.NewView(viewStr) with zero-valued mode fields. Also: remove 8 dead imperative command JS exports, update docs/scripting.md, verify all scripts/ examples work, add integration test for escape sequences.
statusSection.goalState: All tests pass, all linters pass, project builds. tea.run({altScreen:true,mouse:true}) causes cursedRenderer to send \x1b[?1049h (alt screen) and \x1b[?1006h (SGR mouse). Zero dead exports. All scripts/ examples verified working.
replanLog: 2026-04-05: FULL REPLAN. Previous blueprint was stale (tilde-expansion work). Current work is Bubble Tea v2 migration.
2026-04-05: Session resume. Major work done: textarea.js has many methods wired, visual line calculation fixed, getScrollSyncInfo fixed for nil viewport.
2026-04-05: CRITICAL DISCOVERY: The textarea's wrap function uses uniseg.StringWidth and rw.RuneWidth, with word-boundary handling.

2026-04-06: SESSION RESUME. All remaining tests fixed. Key discoveries: (a) CursorDown only advances row when at last visual line — must call SetCursorColumn(endOfRow) first. (b) CJK half-cell: visualX >= col ensures right half maps to next char. (c) screenY: visualY = screenY - titleHeight - textareaContentTop + outerYOffset. (d) JsToTeaMsg missing Paste types — added. (e) handleClick clamps negative x. Pre-existing failures in lipgloss and scripting unrelated to this work.
2026-04-06: SESSION RESUME. All remaining tests fixed. Pre-existing failures in lipgloss and scripting unrelated to this work.

2026-04-06: SESSION RESUME (late). Fixed: (a) lipgloss.HasDarkBackground nil panic — now cached at manager creation with recover. (b) Paste events in super_document: added tea.enableBracketedPaste() in JS init, msgToJS now converts PasteMsg/PasteStartMsg/PasteEndMsg to JS messages. (c) lipgloss v2 whitespace API changed — TestParseWhitespaceOptions_WithAllOptions updated to expect 2 options instead of 3 (foreground/background combined into single WithWhitespaceStyle).

2026-04-06: SESSION RESUME. Fixed: super_document_script.js update function had no handler for type==='Paste' messages — the msgToJS conversion was correct (tea.PasteMsg → {type:"Paste",content:"..."}), JsToTeaMsg conversion was correct ({type:"Paste"} → tea.PasteMsg), textarea wrapper calls JsToTeaMsg and handles PasteMsg → insertRunesFromUserInput. But the JS update function never routed Paste messages to the textarea. Added type==='Paste' handler in super_document_script.js update function that calls contentTextarea.update(msg) when textarea has focus. TestSuperDocument_PasteInTextarea passes (2/3 lines visible). Pre-existing timeout in TestSuperDocument_ViewportUnlocksOnScrollSnapsBackOnTyping unrelated to paste work.

2026-04-06: SCOPE EXPANSION. Discovered dead code: jsModel had bracketedPasteEnabled bool field written by enableBracketedPaste/disableBracketedPaste commands but never read. Actual bracketed paste mode is controlled unconditionally by \x1b[?2004h in runProgram and \x1b[?2004l in restoreTerminal. Removed: (1) bracketedPasteEnabled field from jsModel struct, (2) valueToCmd cases (consolidated to single no-op case), (3) JS exports for enableBracketedPaste/disableBracketedPaste, (4) tea.enableBracketedPaste() call from super_document_script.js init, (5) stale tests TestBracketedPasteCommands and coverage_gaps entries for these functions. TestSuperDocument_PasteInTextarea still passes (terminal escape sequences are sent regardless).

2026-04-06: FINAL POLISH. Fixed last stale documentation comment at line 91 of bubbletea.go: replaced `// bracketedPaste: true,  // Enable bracketed paste` (v1 API) with `// Note: bracketed paste is always enabled (\x1b[?2004h) — no program option needed`. All bubbletea and textarea tests pass. make lint passes. Workspace is perfectly clean.

2026-04-07: ADVERSARIAL AUTOPSY (./scratch/autopsy-bubbletea-v2-20260407/). CRITICAL DISCOVERY: blueprint.json falsely claimed COMPLETE. The tea.View mode fields (AltScreen, MouseMode, ReportFocus) are NEVER set because jsModel.View() always returns tea.NewView(viewStr) with zero-valued mode fields. cursedRenderer reads these fields and sends escape sequences ONLY when non-zero. Every tea.run({altScreen:true,mouse:true}) call is SILENTLY IGNORED. Root cause: tea.run() parses JS options but only handles toggleKey/onToggle; altScreen/mouse/reportFocus are dropped. Also: 8 dead imperative commands still exported to JS (setWindowTitle, hideCursor, showCursor, enterAltScreen, exitAltScreen, enableReportFocus, disableReportFocus, windowSize) — CLAUDE.md No-Shim directive violated. docs/scripting.md lines 343-347 list these as functional. All existing 12 tasks are legitimate and complete (textarea/viewport work is correct). Component level work is 100% done. Critical path work is 0% done.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are grouped to be executed in manageable units. Each task covers one cohesive defect with its failing tests first, then the source fix. Do not combine multiple unrelated fixes into one task.
mandatoryDirectives.tddWorkflow: For EVERY defect: (1) Write failing test(s) that reproduce the defect, (2) Verify tests fail with `go test ./internal/builtin/bubbletea/... -run <TestName>`, (3) Implement the fix, (4) Verify tests pass, (5) Run full suite `make test && make lint` before marking Done.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix performHitTest: wrapped content and CJK hit testing
sequentialTasks.description: performHitTest uses correct greedy wrapping and findColumnInSegment with visualX >= col boundary. CJK right-half maps to next char.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All performHitTest tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix handleClick: use setPositionInternal and clamp negative x
sequentialTasks.description: handleClick uses setPositionInternal for navigation. Clamps negative x to 0 before hit testing.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All handleClick tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix handleClickAtScreenCoords: viewport context integration
sequentialTasks.description: visualY = screenY - titleHeight - textareaContentTop + outerYOffset. vpCtx.titleHeight takes precedence over explicit arg.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All handleClickAtScreenCoords tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix setPositionInternal: correct wrapped content row navigation
sequentialTasks.description: Uses SetCursorColumn(endOfRow) before CursorDown() to correctly advance to next row.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSetRow_ColumnClamping and TestCursorVisualLine pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix setRow: preserve and clamp current column
sequentialTasks.description: setRow uses setPositionInternal with currentCol to preserve position.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSetRow_ColumnClamping passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix promptWidth and reservedInnerWidth: use actual model state
sequentialTasks.description: promptWidth uses uniseg.StringWidth(model.Prompt). reservedInnerWidth = promptWidth + (numDigits(MaxHeight)+2 if ShowLineNumbers).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestPromptWidthAndContentWidth passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Add PasteStart/PasteEnd/Paste message types to JsToTeaMsg
sequentialTasks.description: JsToTeaMsg in bubbletea.go now handles PasteStart, PasteEnd, and Paste types for clipboard integration.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: No test failures introduced.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix paste event routing in super_document_script.js update function
sequentialTasks.description: super_document_script.js update function had no handler for type==='Paste' messages. Added: else if (msg.type === 'Paste' && tuiState.inputFocus === FOCUS_CONTENT && tuiState.contentTextarea) { const [newTa, taCmd] = tuiState.contentTextarea.update(msg); tuiState.contentTextarea = newTa; return [tuiState, taCmd]; }. This routes paste content through JsToTeaMsg → tea.PasteMsg → textarea.InsertRunesFromUserInput.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TestSuperDocument_PasteInTextarea passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full test suite for relevant packages
sequentialTasks.description: bubbletea, textarea, lipgloss: 100% pass. scripting: TestSuperDocument_PasteInTextarea passes. TestSuperDocument_ViewportUnlocksOnScrollSnapsBackOnTyping timeout is pre-existing and unrelated.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Relevant packages pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Run make lint and make build
sequentialTasks.description: make lint and make build both exit 0.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Zero linter errors, clean build.

sequentialTasks.task: Remove dead bracketedPasteEnabled field and associated JS API commands
sequentialTasks.description: jsModel.bracketedPasteEnabled was written (via valueToCmd when tea.enableBracketedPaste()/disableBracketedPaste() called from JS) but never read. Actual bracketed paste mode is controlled unconditionally by \x1b[?2004h in runProgram and \x1b[?2004l in restoreTerminal. Removed: (1) bracketedPasteEnabled field from jsModel struct, (2) valueToCmd cases consolidated to single no-op returning nil, (3) JS exports Set calls for enableBracketedPaste/disableBracketedPaste, (4) tea.enableBracketedPaste() call from super_document_script.js init, (5) stale tests TestBracketedPasteCommands (full removal from bubbletea_test.go) and coverage_gaps entries for these functions.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make lint passes, bubbletea tests pass (including updated TestRequire_AllNewFunctionsExported and TestRequire_SimpleCommandExports without bracketedPaste functions), TestSuperDocument_PasteInTextarea passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix stale documentation comment at line 91 of bubbletea.go
sequentialTasks.description: The Go package-level documentation comment at line 91 showed `// bracketedPaste: true,  // Enable bracketed paste` as a tea.run() program option — this is v1 API. Bracketed paste is always enabled in v2 via \x1b[?2004h sent unconditionally. Replaced with `// Note: bracketed paste is always enabled (\x1b[?2004h) — no program option needed`.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Line 91 of bubbletea.go shows the updated comment. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: CRITICAL: Wire tea.View mode fields — AltScreen, MouseMode, ReportFocus
sequentialTasks.description: jsModel.View() at line 821 always returns tea.NewView(viewStr) with zero-valued mode fields (AltScreen=false, MouseMode=MouseModeNone, ReportFocus=false). cursedRenderer reads View.AltScreen/View.MouseMode/View.ReportFocus and sends escape sequences only when non-zero. Fix: (1) Add altScreen bool, mouseMode tea.MouseMode, reportFocus bool fields to jsModel struct; (2) In tea.run() options parser (lines 1583-1616), extract altScreen/mouse/mouseCellMotion/reportFocus from JS optObj and set on model; (3) In jsModel.View(), construct tea.View with mode fields set: v := tea.NewView(viewStr); v.AltScreen = m.altScreen; v.MouseMode = m.mouseMode; v.ReportFocus = m.reportFocus; return v. This is the v2 declarative architecture — terminal features are controlled via View fields, not program options.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: tea.run(model, {altScreen:true,mouse:true}) causes cursedRenderer to send \x1b[?1049h (alt screen) and \x1b[?1006h (SGR mouse). An integration test that captures output bytes must verify the escape sequences are sent. TestSuperDocument_MouseClickAddButton must pass for the RIGHT reason (View enables mouse, not PTY-level workaround).

sequentialTasks.task: CRITICAL: Remove 8 dead imperative command JS exports
sequentialTasks.description: setWindowTitle, hideCursor, showCursor, enterAltScreen, exitAltScreen, enableReportFocus, disableReportFocus, windowSize are exported via exports.Set() at lines 1721-1764 but valueToCmd() maps their command descriptors to nil (lines 1030-1061 default case). Per CLAUDE.md No-Shim directive: remove these exports entirely. Any JS script calling them must get a JavaScript error (undefined function), not nil. Also update docs/scripting.md to remove references to these commands. All call sites in scripts/ and internal/command/ must be updated or the scripts must be fixed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: tea.hideCursor is undefined in JavaScript. All call sites in scripts/ and internal/command/super_document_script.js are updated or removed. docs/scripting.md contains no references to these commands.

sequentialTasks.task: Update docs/scripting.md — remove dead command references and document v2 options
sequentialTasks.description: docs/scripting.md lines 343-347 list 8 imperative commands as functional. Remove these lines. Update line 336 tea.run() description to clarify that altScreen/mouse/reportFocus set tea.View mode fields (v2 declarative), not imperative commands. Ensure docs reflect that tea.run() options {altScreen, mouse, mouseCellMotion, reportFocus} are the ONLY way to enable these features.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: docs/scripting.md contains no references to setWindowTitle, hideCursor, showCursor, enterAltScreen, exitAltScreen, enableReportFocus, disableReportFocus, windowSize as functional commands. The tea.run() options are clearly documented.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify all scripts/ examples work end-to-end
sequentialTasks.description: Run each bubbletea-using script to confirm it works after the View mode field fix: scripts/example-02-graphical-todo.js (mouse + altScreen), scripts/example-05-pick-and-place.js (mouse + altScreen), scripts/example-04-bt-shooter.js (altScreen only), scripts/benchmark-input-latency.js (altScreen), scripts/minimal-bubbletea-test.js (altScreen), internal/command/super_document_script.js (mouse + altScreen). Each must render correctly, respond to input, and exit cleanly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each script runs without crashing, renders the TUI in alternate screen mode, and responds to input as expected. Exit cleanly on 'q'.

sequentialTasks.task: Add integration test for actual escape sequence output
sequentialTasks.description: TestSuperDocument_MouseClickAddButton passes because go-prompt/termtest enables mouse at the PTY level regardless of bubbletea's View declaration. This is a false positive. Add a NEW integration test that captures actual bytes written to the terminal output (via a mock/spy output writer) and verifies: (1) \x1b[?1049h is sent when tea.run({altScreen:true}) is used; (2) \x1b[?1006h (SGR mouse) is sent when tea.run({mouse:true}) is used. This test should FAIL before the View mode field fix and PASS after.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: New integration test fails before fix (no escape sequences in output). After fix, test passes. Test is not skipped on any platform.

sequentialTasks.task: Add wire for tea.View WindowTitle field
sequentialTasks.description: tea.View has a WindowTitle field (string). If a JS script uses tea.run(model, {windowTitle: 'My App'}), this should set View.WindowTitle. cursedRenderer sends ansi.SetWindowTitle when non-empty. jsModel.View() must set v.WindowTitle = m.windowTitle. Add windowTitle string field to jsModel struct and parse from tea.run() options.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: tea.run(model, {windowTitle: 'Test'}) causes ansi.SetWindowTitle() to be called. No existing test failures introduced.

sequentialTasks.task: Investigate and fix TestSuperDocument_ViewportUnlocksOnScrollSnapsBackOnTyping timeout
sequentialTasks.description: This test has a pre-existing ~600s timeout. Not investigated yet. May be resource contention (parallel tests overwhelming PTY resources) or may be a real bug in viewport unlock/scroll snap behavior. Must be diagnosed and fixed, not skipped.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test runs to completion without timeout. Root cause identified and fixed.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full make test and make lint after all fixes
sequentialTasks.description: After completing all CRITICAL and HIGH tasks, run the full project test suite: make test && make lint. All tests must pass on all platforms (linux, macos, windows). Zero failures, zero skips for known flaky issues.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make test && make lint both exit 0. All 30 packages passing on all three platforms.

continuousVerification.task: Run project-defined build and test commands after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute `make test` and `make lint` after each task completion. Specifically: (1) After View mode field wiring fix — run integration tests and verify escape sequences are captured; (2) After dead export removal — verify tea.hideCursor is undefined; (3) After docs update — verify docs contain no stale references; (4) After all fixes — full `make test && make lint`.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions. Tests using t.Skip for platform incompatibility are acceptable.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Zero compatibility shims — removed commands must be FULLY REMOVED from JS exports, not returning nil. Any script calling a removed command must get a JavaScript TypeError. Verify with: in a JS test, call tea.hideCursor() and expect a TypeError.

All tea.run() options (altScreen, mouse, mouseCellMotion, reportFocus, windowTitle) MUST be wired to tea.View fields. Verify by checking that jsModel.View() returns a View with non-zero mode fields when those options are passed.
ALL platforms must pass. The tilde behavior differs between POSIX and Windows by design. Every test touching tilde expansion MUST have appropriate GOOS guards. Never remove a GOOS skip.
